methodsAttempted.notes: Checked official robots policies for www.napier.ac.uk, my.napier.ac.uk, and documentcentre.napier.ac.uk before fetching official sources.

methodsAttempted.query: site:napier.ac.uk "generative AI" "Edinburgh Napier" policy students academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found a My Napier student guidance page, an official generative AI position PDF, an Assessment Policy PDF, a low-specificity people profile, and an internal search-results page.
Firecrawl search returned only a My Napier search-results page, so it was documented as low-specificity rather than used as evidence.
Official documentcentre PDFs were fetched over HTTP and converted to normalized text locally for short evidence snippets and hashes.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a student-facing My Napier generative AI guidance page and two official documentcentre PDFs with institution-level assessment and generative AI position text. A staff profile and a My Napier search-results page were rejected because they are not policy-specific evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence tools and your learning
Edinburgh Napier University's Position on Generative Artificial Intelligence: Our Shared Commitment to Ethical and Responsible Use

stopConditions: Stop once official institution-level sources support narrow claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide policy from staff profile, search result, research showcase, or ranking metadata.
Keep all claim candidates agent_reviewed and non-canonical.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence tools and your learning
Edinburgh Napier University's Position on Generative Artificial Intelligence: Our Shared Commitment to Ethical and Responsible Use

artifacts.claimText: Edinburgh Napier University's Assessment Policy states that inappropriate use of Generative AI in assessment, including presenting generated content as a student's own work, is cheating and a breach of the Academic Integrity Regulations.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: students' inappropriate use of Generative AI, such as the direct use of generated content presented in an assessment submission as their own work, constitutes a form of cheating and a breach of the Academic Integrity Regulations.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 3, Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Assessment
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Edinburgh Napier University's Assessment Policy says programme and module leaders will discuss students' use of generative AI and use the AI Toolkit traffic-light system and assessment briefs to indicate what use is appropriate.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Programme and module leaders will engage in dialogue with their students on their use of generative AI, using the AI Toolkit and its traffic-light system to indicate what use of generative AI is appropriate in their modules and assessments. Staff will use the assessment brief to indicate what use of Generative AI is appropriate in any given assessment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 3, Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Assessment
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Edinburgh Napier University's Assessment Policy says every assessment will use the Assessment Declaration Cover Sheet for students to declare that the submission is their own work and acknowledge other-source contributions, including Generative AI use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Assessment Declaration Cover Sheet will be used in every assessment for students to declare that their submission is their own work and that any contributions from other sources have been acknowledged, including any use of Generative AI.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 3, Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Assessment
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Edinburgh Napier University's generative AI position states that the university does not permit Gen AI detection tools on student assessment submissions, and academic staff are not permitted to use external detection tools on student work.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Edinburgh Napier University's Position on Generative Artificial Intelligence: Our Shared Commitment to Ethical and Responsible Use
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University Leadership does not permit the use of Gen AI detection tools on student assessment submissions, as current technologies have proven to be unreliable, prone to bias, and we do not have students' informed consent for their use with their work.

artifacts.claimText: Edinburgh Napier University's generative AI position says academic staff should never require students to sign up for or use Gen AI tools, citing privacy, ethical, and environmental concerns, and should provide an alternative means of engaging with the task.

artifacts.notes: Narrow claim directly supported by official Assessment Policy section 3; not marked canonical or human reviewed.
Assessment-brief and traffic-light claim is limited to the official Assessment Policy wording.
Declaration claim is directly scoped to the Assessment Declaration Cover Sheet text in the policy.
Detection-tools claim is directly supported by the university position PDF and avoids broader tool-governance inferences.
Student acknowledgement claim uses the official position statement's undergraduate and taught postgraduate student section.
Claim is limited to staff guidance on not requiring student sign-up/use of Gen AI tools and providing alternatives.
Student-facing My Napier page supports a narrow module-leader/tutor guidance claim.

artifacts.title: Edinburgh Napier University Generative AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Draft staged evidence for Edinburgh Napier University identifies official guidance and policy text on Gen AI in assessment, acknowledgement and declaration expectations, AI detection tools, and not requiring students to use Gen AI tools. Claims remain agent-reviewed candidates pending downstream review.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Rejected official search-result and people-profile pages were not used as policy evidence because they did not contain institution-level policy text.
Documentcentre PDFs were used only for short metadata snippets; source documents retain their original rights and terms.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
